title: Our Course Project
text: Spatial Risk Assessment and Urban Transformation Prioritization for Istanbul: A Data-Driven Approach

title: Our Course Project
text:
Yusuf Bora Çavdar
Onur Furkan Gök

title: Our Course Project
text: The primary objective of this project is to develop a Decision Support Model to identify and prioritize neighborhoods in Istanbul for urban transformation. By integrating building age, official structural needs, and population density, the project aims to calculate a “Seismic Vulnerability Index.” This model will help in optimizing the allocation of urban renewal resources to the areas with the highest potential impact on human life and safety.

title: Our Course Project
text:
The analysis will be grounded on three primary datasets sourced from the IBB Open Data Portal and TUIK:

Building Inventory Dataset: Contains neighborhood-level data on the number of buildings categorized by construction year (pre-1980, 1980-2000, post-2000) and floor counts.
Urban Transformation Requirement Data: Provides official records of buildings identified as “In Need of Transformation” vs. “Not in Need” across districts.
Municipal Population Dataset (2019-2024): A time-series dataset showing population distribution and density across Istanbul’s 39 districts.

title: Our Course Project
text:
The project will follow a structured Data Science workflow:

Data Preprocessing: Cleaning and merging the datasets using dplyr and tidyr. Handling “Wide” to “Long” format conversions for population trends.
Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA): Visualizing the distribution of aged buildings and population hotspots using ggplot2.
Index Calculation: Implementing a weighted risk formula to score each neighborhood/district:
RiskIndex​=(Age_Factor×0.4)+(Transformation_Need×0.3)+(Population_Density×0.3)
Final Output: A dynamic Quarto report hosted on GitHub Pages, featuring an interactive risk ranking and priority map of Istanbul.

title: Yusuf Bora Çavdar's Analytics Lab
text:
Assignment 1 – Task 3
Video Summary & Data Analysis
Industrial Engineering and Data Science integration based on Cem Vardar’s interview.


Summary
I watched the interview with Cem Vardar, who holds a PhD from Arizona State University and has worked at companies such as Intel and Carvana.
The interview mainly focuses on the relationship between Industrial Engineering and Data Science in real-world applications.
According to Vardar, Industrial Engineering is fundamentally about solving problems within complex systems, while Data Science should be viewed as a practical tool for business problem-solving rather than a standalone profession.
One of the most important concepts mentioned in the interview is “Evolutionary Design,” which emphasizes improving systems gradually through small, data-driven changes instead of replacing entire systems at once.
He also highlights that foundational tools such as SQL and Excel remain highly valuable despite rapid technological changes.


Questions


Open-Ended Question

What is Cem Vardar’s “Evolutionary Design” approach, and why does he suggest it instead of replacing an entire system?

Answer
It refers to improving systems incrementally through small and manageable changes. This approach is preferred because complete system replacements are often expensive, risky, and difficult to implement successfully in real business environments.



Multiple-Choice Question

Which tool is described as a “must-have” skill for over 20 years?

Neural Networks
Deep Fakes
SQL (Structured Query Language)
ChatGPT 

Answer

SQL




AI Disclosure
I used Gemini 3 Flash to:


Summarize the interview transcript


Format the questions


Improve clarity and readability


I also used Gemini AI to debug and correct errors in my R code.


Video Link 
Watch the Interview on YouTube



Data Analysis Section
In this section, the polls_us_election_2016 dataset from the dslabs package is analyzed using R.
library(dslabs) library(dplyr)
data(“polls_us_election_2016”) data <- polls_us_election_2016
my_first <- “Bora” my_birth_year <- 2004
k <- (nchar(my_first) + my_birth_year) %% 15 + 8 k
if (k %% 2 == 0) { head(data, k) } else { tail(data, k) }
sum(is.na(data))
na_per_column <- sort(colSums(is.na(data)), decreasing = TRUE) head(na_per_column, 8)
new_data <- data %>% mutate( across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), my_birth_year + k, .)), across(where(is.character), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), paste0(my_first, “”, k), .)), across(where(is.factor), ~ { x <- as.character(.) x[is.na(x)] <- paste0(my_first, ””, k) x }) )
if (k %% 2 == 0) { head(new_data, k) } else { tail(new_data, k) }
sum(is.na(new_data)) anyNA(new_data)


Built with R and Quarto

```

Research Design · Data Processing · Methodological Development

title: Project Phase 1
text:
Problem Statement & Motivation
While university placement statistics are publicly available, there is often a lack of analytical perspective explaining why certain departments consistently outperform others in national rankings.
As Industrial Engineering students, our objective is to apply modern data analytics methodologies directly to our own academic domain.
We aim to identify the underlying drivers of success and regional demand for Industrial Engineering departments across Turkey over recent years.

title: Project Phase 1
text:

YÖK Atlas – Lisans Atlası

thestats R Package
Developed by M. Çavuş and O. Aydın

National base rankings
Program quotas
Demand ratios
Faculty indicators
Erasmus mobility
Student regional origins



Temporal Coverage

2018 – 2025

title: Project Phase 1
text:
Proposed Methodology
The analysis is implemented using R (thestats, tidyverse, ggplot2) and reported through Quarto for full reproducibility.

Analytical Workflow

Data extraction and harmonization
Missing value handling and preprocessing
Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
Trend analysis and sector benchmarking
Correlation modeling
Interactive visualization and reporting

title: Project Phase 1
text:
Expected Project Output
The final deliverable will be a fully reproducible analytical website hosted through GitHub Pages. The platform will include:

Interactive visual analytics
Historical ranking trend exploration
Sector comparison dashboards
Correlation heatmaps
Integrated .RData reproducible datasets
Advanced Quarto-based reporting infrastructure

title: Project Phase 1
text:
Research Motivation
University placement statistics in Turkey are publicly available, yet raw admission numbers alone fail to explain why certain Industrial Engineering departments consistently outperform others in national rankings.
As Industrial Engineering students, our objective is to apply analytical methodologies directly to our own academic domain. This project investigates how institutional capacity, regional location, academic mobility, and demand intensity collectively shape departmental competitiveness across Turkey.
Rather than treating placement data as isolated yearly statistics, we aim to construct a unified analytical framework capable of identifying the structural drivers behind academic prestige and student demand.

title: Project Phase 1
text:
Data Sources
Our analytical pipeline integrates multiple educational datasets into a reproducible research workflow.







Source
Purpose




YÖK Atlas – Lisans Atlası
Official placement statistics and ranking data


thestats R Package
Direct programmatic extraction into R


Institutional Indicators
Erasmus mobility, faculty size, demand metrics




Core Variables

National base rankings
Program quotas
Demand-to-quota ratios
Faculty size indicators
Erasmus exchange mobility
Student regional origin distributions

title: Project Phase 1
text:
Methodological Framework
The project is implemented entirely in R using reproducible data science workflows.

Analytical Pipeline

Data Extraction & Harmonization
Multi-year Industrial Engineering program data collected and standardized.
Data Cleaning & Feature Engineering
Missing value handling, normalization, and derived metric generation.
Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
Trend analysis, institutional benchmarking, and ranking shifts.
Correlation Modeling
Measuring relationships between academic capacity, mobility, demand, and ranking performance.
Interactive Reporting
Visualization and reporting infrastructure developed through Quarto.

title: Project Phase 1
text:
Expected Deliverables
The final project output will include:

Interactive analytical visualizations
Historical ranking trend exploration
Sector comparison dashboards
Correlation heatmaps
Downloadable .RData datasets
Reproducible Quarto reporting infrastructure

title: Project Phase 1
text:
Project Overview
This project investigates the structural and institutional factors shaping the success of Industrial Engineering departments across Turkey between 2018 and 2025.
Rather than treating placement statistics as isolated yearly figures, we aim to construct an integrated analytical framework capable of identifying the underlying drivers of academic prestige, student demand, and regional competitiveness.
